# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

# The Cloud Deployment Manager configuration file (deployer/configs) generated by deploymanager_v1beta platform for the following example to create a new Google Compute Engine Instance.  You can customize this as per requirement or use any other method of managing your deployment tasks in GCP environment, and must follow best practices when defining these configuration files such that they are securely version-controlled if you're using GitHub for source control purposes:
apiVersion: v2
kind: DeploymentManagerConfigTemplate
metadata:  # Template parameters can be defined as metadata here.   Example : - name="mydeployments/${NAME}/instances1"/-->templateName=$(name)/instance-%senv,location[default]= $LOCATION -->  labels["cloud_provider"] = 'gcp'
  annotations: {}
spec: # Configuration for the template in YAML format. The Cloud Deployment Manager will use this to manage and launch instances based on it.   Example : - properties/name=${NAME}/instances1;properties[metadata][self-link]="$(REF_LINK)" -->templateName = $TEMPLATEPATH
  template: | # Template for the Deployment Manager instance that will be deployed to GCP using gcloud command. This is a declarative format which defines how instances of this kind are created and deleted in Cloud Compute Engine; example : - labels = { "env"=kubernetes-${ENV}, … }; properties/name = $NAME, location[default]= $LOCATION -->templateName=$TEMPLATEPATH
    spec: | # Configuration for the actual instance to be deployed. This is an inline format which describes how instances of this kind are created in Cloud Compute Engine; example : - metadata = {"startup-script"="#!/bin/sh\n echo 'Hello, world!' > /helloworld.txt", "labels":{"env":"kubernetes-"${ENV} }}; ...
---  # End of DeploymentManagerConfigTemplate definition ---   Template end marker (-----) ----- The above config file should be copied to gcloud's resource manager and pasted into the following command line for deployment. e.g., `cat ./deployer/configs/* | gcloud -q compute instances create myproject --zone=us-central1
